h2h_summary: Croatia and Colombia have met only once in recorded history, with Croatia winning 2-1 in a March 2026 international friendly in Orlando.
team_style_home: Colombia operate in a 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3 under Nestor Lorenzo, combining a compact defensive block with fast vertical counter-attacks, using the pace of Luis Diaz to exploit half-spaces and the creativity of James Rodriguez to unlock deep defences.
team_style_away: Croatia under Zlatko Dalic favour a 4-3-3 or 3-4-2-1 built around midfield control and technical possession, with Luka Modric orchestrating from a No.10 role and full-backs providing width in quick transitions from defence to attack.
wc_history_home: Colombia's finest World Cup campaign was in 2014, where they reached the quarter-finals for the first time and James Rodriguez won the Golden Boot with six goals; the 2026 tournament marks their seventh World Cup appearance.
wc_history_away: Croatia have won three World Cup medals — third place in 1998 on debut, runners-up in 2018 (losing 4-2 to France in the final), and third place again in 2022 — making them the most decorated small nation in World Cup history.
group_context: This Round of 32 match (Match 83) pits the second-placed finisher from Group K (Portugal, Colombia, DR Congo, Uzbekistan) against the second-placed finisher from Group L (England, Croatia, Ghana, Panama), with the winner advancing to the Round of 16; predictions point to Colombia as the Group K runner-up behind Portugal, and Croatia as the Group L runner-up behind England.
strengths_home: Elite attacking pace and creativity through Luis Diaz, James Rodriguez, and Jhon Arias
Strong defensive compactness conceding only 15 goals across 18 CONMEBOL qualifying matches
Excellent recent form with six wins from eight pre-tournament matches
strengths_away: World-class midfield control and tempo-setting through Modric and Kovacic
Exceptional big-tournament pedigree with three World Cup medals in 1998, 2018, and 2022
Tactical flexibility between 4-3-3 and 3-4-2-1 depending on opponent
